australia.title: 'Extremely corrosive': Trump called out for remarks aimed at Australia
summary: Summarize this news article:
The senior US Democrat on a Congressional committee overseeing AUKUS says US President Donald Trump's repeated criticism of Australia is "unfair" and causing harm to the US with its allies.
"I think it's a very unfair criticism by the president," Joe Courtney told 7.30.

Mr Trump said on two occasions he was "not happy"with Australia's position on the war in Iran, saying Australia had declined a request to join US efforts in the Strait of Hormuz, something the Albanese government has denied.
Democrat Joe Courtney is on a Congressional committee overseeing AUKUS.(Getty Images: Bill Clark)
Mr Courtney, who is also the founder and co-chair of the bipartisan Friends of Australia Caucus, says Mr Trump's attitude is damaging.

Asked whether he was surprised to hear that members of the Coalition — traditionally unambiguous supporters of the US — were being critical of Mr Trump's comments, Mr Courtney said: "I'm not surprised to hear that unfortunately, because we obviously follow what's going o Summarize this news article:
n in Australia … and we're not blind to the fact that this harm and damage that's being done is affecting public opinion."
A US ship fires a missile during the Iran war in this image provided by US Central Command.(US Navy via AP)
Mr Courtney has been a persistent critic of the war in Iran, calling for Congressional oversight of the deployment of troops to the Middle East and the Gulf.
He also believes Mr Trump's inconsistent public statements are a problem.
"We can't just keep giving him [President Trump] free reign to conduct this war, Mr Courtney said.

"He's changed his mind almost on a daily basis about whether or not we've won, we've lost; whether or not it's hours away or days away for a settlement. I mean, we clearly are not at that place right now.
US Navy guided-missile destroyers are being used for the Strait of Hormuz blockade.(US Central Command)
Mr Courtney says Americans are currently experiencing the negative consequences of the war in terms of fuel and fertiliser prices

australia.title: Acts of courage during Wieambilla tragedy honoured with police bravery awards
summary: Summarize this news article:
More than 290 people, including the two officers who survived the deadly Wieambilla shooting tragedy, have been recognised with Queensland police bravery awards.
Constables Rachel McCrow, Matthew Arnold, Keely Brough and Senior Constable Randall Kirk attended the remote Western Downs property in response to a missing person's report in 2022.
The ambush unfolded at the Trains' remote Western Downs property in December, 2012.(ABC News)
Within minutes of entering the property, Constables McCrow and Arnold were fatally shot.
Gareth, Stacey and Nathaniel Train carried out the deadly attack and were fatally shot by police after several hours of negotiations.
Neighbour Alan Dare was also killed during the hours-long siege.
Queensland Police officers Matthew Arnold and Rachel McCrow and neighbour Alan Dare were killed at Wieambilla in 2022.(Supplied)
Commissioner Brett Pointing said the tragedy remained deeply etched in the hearts of the Queensland Police Service (QPS) and the community.
"Toda Summarize this news article:
y we recognise the extraordinary acts of courage, professionalism and selflessness demonstrated by members of the QPS and partner agencies," he said at a private ceremony held in Dalby on Wednesday.
Commissioner Brett Pointing said the tragedy remained deeply etched in the hearts of the Queensland Police Service and the community.(Supplied: Queensland Police Service)
The first recipient of the awards was Senior Constable Kirk, who was honoured with a valour and blue heart medal.
When presenting the award, Superintendent Douglas McDonald said Senior Constable Kirk had demonstrated exceptional valour and composure under extreme threat.
He said despite the senior constable sustaining serious injury, he maintained critical observations and relayed vital intelligence while under fire from heavily armed offenders.
Senior Constable Kirk was honoured with a valour and blue heart medal for exceptional valour and composure under extreme threat.(Supplied: Queensland Police Service)

australia.title: Three Melbourne teens charged over alleged attempted kidnapping
summary: Summarize this news article:

Police have charged three teenagers over a string of alleged violent crimes, including attempted kidnapping, with investigators exploring links to a recent series of arson attacks on Melbourne hospitality venues.

The three 17-year-olds are alleged to have targeted the promoters of a weekly Saturday night event at a licensed venue in South Melbourne in what they described as brazen attacks over six days of violence.
The trio are accused of an attempted kidnapping at a Templestowe home on April 9, before returning the next night when they allegedly carried out an aggravated home invasion.
Police then say the three teens tried to commit an aggravated carjacking in Wollert on April 12, before attempting two separate aggravated home invasions the next day at the same address.
The following day, April 14, the three boys are accused of conducting an aggravated home invasion and kidnapping at a Malvern address.
Police believe the Malvern attack was targeted, but occurred at the wrong address.
Summarize this news article:

The trio have also been charged separately with a litany of other offences, including committing an indictable offence while on bail, possession of a controlled weapon, possession of methylamphetamine, and motor vehicle theft.
A specialist police operation is investigating whether recent arson attacks across Melbourne's hospitality venues are linked.
Police executed search warrants at three properties in Melbourne's north-west on Wednesday morning, taking the three into custody and charging them.
The three boys will face a children's court at a later date.
Investigators are now working with the Operation Eclipse taskforce to determine whether the alleged incidents are linked toa wider series of attacks on Melbourne hospitality venues in recent weeks.
Restaurateurs, publicans and hospitality operators across the city have awoken during April to discover their businesses damaged by suspicious fires.
Since April 14, Victoria Police has identified 15 separate arson or attempted arsons it b

india.title: 'This is TMC’s technique': BJP alleges party symbol 'taped' over in Bengal's Falta
summary: Summarize this news article:

NEW DELHI: The BJP on Wednesday alleged irregularities during the second phase of the West Bengal assembly elections, claiming that its party symbol had been taped over on electronic voting machines (EVMs) at several polling booths in Falta under the Diamond Harbour constituency.

Leader of opposition and BJP candidate from Bhabanipur and Nandigram, Suvendu Adhikari said he had spoken to senior leaders, including Union home minister Amit Shah and BJP leader Bhupendra Yadav, about the alleged malpractice. âThe Election Commission has taken cognisance. If it works in this manner, then what is required will be done,â he said, noting that only around 35 per cent of voting had taken place at that point.

BJP IT cell head Amit Malviya claimed that voters were prevented from selecting the BJP option in multiple booths due to the use of tape. He labelled the alleged practice the âDiamond Harbour Modelâ and called for an immediate repoll in affected areas. Malviya also cited specific boot Summarize this news article:
hs, including one at Harindanga High School, and claimed that several others, such as booth 189, had been similarly compromised.
Responding to the claims, the West Bengal Chief Electoral Officer said any reports of tampering would be thoroughly verified.
âIf reports of taping of any button come in, that should be verified and noted. If true, those booths will go for a repoll,â the CEO said, assuring action if irregularities were confirmed.

The controversy unfolded amid brisk polling across 142 constituencies in the second phase, with voter turnout reaching nearly 40 per cent by 11 am. Districts such as Purba Bardhaman and Hooghly recorded some of the highest participation levels.
Meanwhile, chief minister Mamata Banerjee also raised concerns over the conduct of central forces and polling authorities, alleging that ânew peopleâ brought in were acting arbitrarily.

india.title: 'No vacuum in law to deal with the offence' — What Supreme Court said on hate speeches
summary: Summarize this news article:
stated on Wednesday that existing legal provisions are sufficient to address hate speech offences, declining to issue further directions on the matter as it falls within the legislature's domain.
A bench of Justice Vikram Nath and Justice Sandeep Mehta delivered the judgment on a batch of petitions seeking directions and guidelines to address the issue of hate speech.

"Prescription of punishment lies squarely within legislative domain. Constitutional scheme founded upon doctrine of separation of powers does not permit judiciary to create new offences," the Supreme Court said, adding that no legislative vacuum exists to warrant the intervention sought.
The court, however, urged the Centre to examine whether fresh legislation is warranted to tackle hate speech in light of changing social contexts.
While declining to issue the directions sought in the petitions, the bench observed that existing criminal laws already address offences related to hate speech.
"The precedents of this court co Summarize this news article:

nsistently affirm that while Constitutional courts may interpret the law and issue directions to secure the enforcement of fundamental rights, they cannot legislate or compel legislation," the bench said.

The bench said it would be open to the Centre and other competent legislative authorities to consider whether additional legal or policy measures are needed in view of evolving societal challenges, including making suitable amendments as suggested in the Law Commissionâs 267th report of March 2017. one over over over had their their their well well well their well their over over one their one our over over their their over had one over one over well their their one Their one our their one over Their their their our over had our their their

india.title: From Riyan Parag to ABD: Cricketers seen vaping on camera
summary: Summarize this news article:
has found himself at the centre of a major controversy after visuals of him allegedly vaping inside the dressing room during their
2026 clash against Punjab Kings went viral on social media.

The incident occurred during the 16th over of Rajasthanâs run chase, when Parag was seen casually vaping while seated alongside teammates Dhruv Jurel and Yashasvi Jaiswal. The footage, captured during the live broadcast, quickly spread online and sparked widespread debate.

The act has raised serious concerns, particularly because smoking is strictly prohibited inside dressing rooms and stadium premises, except in designated areas. More importantly, e-cigarettes and vapes are banned in India under the Prohibition of Electronic Cigarettes Act (PECA), 2019, which prohibits their production, sale, purchase, and use. Violations can attract penalties, including fines and imprisonment.
This is not the first time Rajasthan Royals have been embroiled in controversy this season. Earlier, team manager Romi  Summarize this news article:
Bhinder was warned and fined after being caught using a phone in the dugout, in violation of PMOA protocol.
Paragâs case also adds to a growing list of cricketers who have been caught in similar situations over the years.

Back in 2020, a short clip of Aaron Finch vaping during an IPL match between Rajasthan Royals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru went viral. The Ex-Australian captain was seen exhaling smoke and taking another puff before the broadcast cut back to live action, but the moment was enough to spread rapidly across social media.

More recently, during an SA20 match earlier this year, cameras captured AB de Villiers vaping in the VIP stands. The footage caused a stir, with the former South African star appearing visibly startled after realising he was on the big screen.

While those incidents involved vaping, there have also been cases of players admitting to smoking. Former New Zealand captain Brendon McCullum revealed he smoked in a toilet during the 2015 World Cup semi-fina

india.title: ‘Merci, Dhanyavaad…’: Google CEO Sundar Pichai sends message to 1 billion users
summary: Summarize this news article:

is celebrating the 20th anniversary of Google Translate, a tool that has evolved from a niche research project in 2006 into a globally used artificial intelligence (AI) tool by over 1 billion. Marking the occasion, Google CEO

reflected on the platform's journey from supporting just a handful of languages at launch to nearly 250 languages today. The company emphasised that the core mission has remained unchanged: helping people understand one another across borders.

What started as a small internal experiment has transformed into a sophisticated ecosystem that includes live conversation translation, image-based translation via Google Lens, and real-time transcription.
